video:video.video:title: Faithful find joy and importance in Pope Leo's visit to mountainous Montserrat abbey
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV participated in the reading of the rosary at an ancient mountain-top abbey in Montserrat on Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: New York fans celebrate as Knicks complete record rally, take 3-1 NBA Finals lead
video:video.video:description: Massive crowds filled streets in Manhattan after the NBA New York Knicks completed a record comeback, defeating the San Antonio Spurs 107-106 in Game 4 of the NBA Finals.

video:video.video:title: FIFA's Infantino says Iran, ticketing and visas are main issues on eve of World Cup
video:video.video:description: On the eve of the start of the World Cup FIFA's president acknowledge three main concerns outside of soccer's realm which had been dominating headlines ahead of the tournament.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories June 11
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Thursday, June 11th: U.S. launches second round of airstrikes across Iran; Iran says Strait of Hormuz is closed; Tens of thousands displaced after Philippines earthquake; Knicks beat Spurs in Game Four of NBA Finals.

video:video.video:title: Hong Kong customs swoop ahead of the World Cup, seizing $20M in fake goods
video:video.video:description: Hong Kong authorities announced, just hours before the World Cup was set to kick off, the seizure of 230,000 suspected counterfeit items worth an estimated $20 million, including jerseys tied to the highly anticipated soccer tournament.

video:video.video:title: An uncertain future for ‘Doctor Who’
video:video.video:description: What next for "Doctor Who"? The British broadcaster has canceled a Christmas special previously announced for later this year, and showrunner Russell T.

video:video.video:title: Postcard helps identify British soldier and track down his family a century after death in WWI
video:video.video:description: A postcard belonging to a World War I soldier whose body was found with five comrades during an excavation has helped reunite distant descendants more than a century after his death on the Western Front.

video:video.video:title: Feeling the heat: How extreme temperatures could impact the World Cup
video:video.video:description: As the FIFA World Cup kicks off, concerns are emerging over the impact of extreme temperatures on both players and spectators.

video:video.video:title: Northern English town set for by-election which may give Andy Burnham a path to be next UK leader
video:video.video:description: Nearly 75,000 voters in Ashton-in-Makerfield will cast ballots in a contest that may well pick the UK’s next prime minister, or plunge Britain's febrile politics into even more turmoil.

video:video.video:title: At 86, Al Pacino reflects
video:video.video:description: Al Pacino looks back as he promotes “In the Hand of Dante" - his second film at Tribeca.

video:video.video:title: AI-powered radar helps avoid conflict with polar bears in Canada
video:video.video:description: Arctic sea ice is shrinking from climate change forcing many polar bears have to shift their diets to land during parts of the summer.

video:video.video:title: Sagrada Familia lights up as Pope Leo blesses the world's tallest church in Spain
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV has celebrated the Sagrada Familia Basilica as a masterpiece of “stones, colors and light.”

video:video.video:title: New York fans celebrate Knicks' historic Game 4 comeback win
video:video.video:description: The New York Knicks made a record comeback from 29 points down and moved to the brink of their first championship since 1973 by beating the San Antonio Spurs 107-106 in Game 4 of the NBA Finals.

video:video.video:title: 'PONIES' explores the Cold War with a twist
video:video.video:description: Stars Emilia Clarke and Haley Lu Richardson discuss the difficulties of speaking Russian and donning 1970s clothes to play widows-turned-spies in hit series "PONIES."

video:video.video:title: Two-day-old baby elephant born in UK zoo plays with ball on eve of World Cup
video:video.video:description: A baby Asian elephant made his debut at a UK zoo on Wednesday at just two days old. On the eve of the FIFA World Cup, the baby boy was given a ball to play with and could be seen attempting a few dribbles and investigating with his trunk.

video:video.video:title: Soccer offers West Bank Palestinians an escape as the World Cup kicks off
video:video.video:description: As the FIFA World Cup gets underway, with Egypt, Iraq, Jordan, Qatar and Saudi Arabia among the Arab nations set to compete, soccer remains both a passion and a struggle for many Palestinians in the occupied West Bank.

video:video.video:title: House vote fails to extend FISA spy tool as Friday deadline looms
video:video.video:description: The impasse could soon result in limitations on what intelligence the U.S. government can collect abroad just as World Cup games begin in cities around the country and ahead of celebrations for the nation’s 250th anniversary.

video:video.video:title: El Nino has formed in the Pacific and could set records, forecasters say
video:video.video:description: The weather pattern's effects vary by region. El Nino often dampens — but doesn't eliminate — Atlantic hurricane season activity, but increases it in the Pacific.

video:video.video:title: World Cup to kick off in Mexico City as fans celebrate and protesters take to the streets
video:video.video:description: Mexico launches the 2026 World Cup before a global audience as President Claudia Sheinbaum faces a high-profile test as protesters take to the streets.

video:video.video:title: Worry mixed with skepticism over possibility of war ending among residents of Iran's capital
video:video.video:description: Tehran residents expressed mixed views on Thursday over more U.S. airstrikes and the prospects of an agreement. (AP video shot by Mohsen Ganji)

video:video.video:title: Taylor Swift at Knicks vs Spurs
video:video.video:description: Stars including Taylor Swift and A$AP Rocky attend Knicks' record-breaking rally over Spurs in Game 4 of the NBA Final.

video:video.video:title: Homeland security secretary defends decision on World Cup referee from Somalia
video:video.video:description: Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin is defending his department's decision to deny a World Cup referee from Somalia entry to the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Storms knock out power in the Midwest, damage buildings and disrupt flights
video:video.video:description: Damaging storms are sweeping through the Midwest, disrupting flights at Chicago airports and knocking out power to hundreds of thousands of customers.

video:video.video:title: Matt Damon raps to fight global water crisis
video:video.video:description: Matt Damon discusses his mission to bring clean and accessible water to communities around the world with the Get Blue project. (June 11)

video:video.video:title: US launches a second day of strikes on Iran, Iran fires back at the Gulf states and Jordan
video:video.video:description: The United States launched a second round of airstrikes on Iran into Thursday morning after U.S. President Donald Trump warned that Tehran would “pay the price” for stalled negotiations.

video:video.video:title: Foreign workers say they were paid less than $2 an hour to build new US consulate in Milan
video:video.video:description: Italian prosecutors are investigating Alabama-based Caddell Construction after workers allege labor exploitation, according to The Associated Press interviews with five former employees and a review of their employment letters and pay stubs.

video:video.video:title: India's viral Cockroach Party launches nationwide youth protests
video:video.video:description: India’s viral Cockroach Janta Party launched a nationwide protest campaign Thursday. The group is calling for the education minister's resignation after alleged examination irregularities.

video:video.video:title: What stars say you can't have the World Cup without
video:video.video:description: You can't have a World Cup without... tequila? Here's what the stars think.

video:video.video:title: What can LA soccer fans do if they don't have World Cup tickets?
video:video.video:description: What can Los Angeles soccer fans do if they don't have World Cup tickets? AP reporter Sarah Jones-Smith takes you inside the official fan festival.

video:video.video:title: India lodges 'strong' protest with US after tanker strike kills three mariners
video:video.video:description: India's foreign ministry said New Delhi lodged a "strong protest" with the United States after three Indian seafarers were killed in a U.S. military strike on a tanker in the Gulf of Oman on Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Agriculture Sec. Rollins says sterile fly program working against screwworm in Texas
video:video.video:description: U.S. Agriculture Secretary Brooke Rollins watched sterile flies being released Thursday to fight the New World screwworm and visited a Texas ranch where one of the first cases of the pest was detected.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories June 11
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Thursday, June 11th: Trump backs off new strikes against Iran; Labor Department reports big jump in producer prices; Trump announces new pick for director of national intelligence; World Cup fever strikes Mexico as tournament begins.

video:video.video:title: Lukas Van Ness discusses the trajectory of his career
video:video.video:description: Green Bay Packers edge rusher Lukas Van Ness discusses the trajectory of his career since getting selected in the first round of the 2023 draft.

video:video.video:title: US stocks jump to their best day in 2 months on hopes for a deal to get crude flowing globally again
video:video.video:description: U.S. stocks rallied to their best day in two months, and oil prices fell Thursday after President Donald Trump called off his threat to bomb Iran in the evening.

video:video.video:title: Kansas City fan festival welcomes fans for the opening match of the World Cup
video:video.video:description: Soccer fans flocked to Kansas City’s FIFA World Cup Festival watch the opening match of the tournament between Mexico and South Africa. (AP video by Nick Ingram)

video:video.video:title: Police officer in Toronto killed in shooting linked to investigation of a US Consulate attack
video:video.video:description: One 19-year-old suspect was in custody at a hospital in critical condition while officers searched for a second suspect, who was identified as 19-year-old Zara Jabbi and was considered armed and dangerous.

video:video.video:title: World Cup begins in Mexico City
video:video.video:description: The largest World Cup in tournament history kicks off with co-host Mexico taking on South Africa in the opening match. Here's how fans feel.

video:video.video:title: Canadian PM says officials working through issues after Canada-US bridge opening delay
video:video.video:description: The opening of a Canadian-U.S. bridge across the Detroit River, which President Donald Trump had previously threatened to block, has been delayed due to unresolved issues.

video:video.video:title: Pope highlights migrants' plight, urges humility, on Canary Islands
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV has traveled to the Canary Islands, an epicenter of the European migration debate. He challenged countries to uphold migrants’ rights while shaming those leaders, including Christians, who turn them away with indifference.

video:video.video:title: Paul Stanley credits teamwork for the lasting success of KISS
video:video.video:description: Stanley says "the love of the band" helps sustain his working relationship with Gene Simmons. (June 11)

video:video.video:title: Fire at medical equipment warehouse in California prompts evacuations at nearby warehouses
video:video.video:description: A fire at a medical equipment warehouse in Northern California sent thick black smoke pouring into the sky Thursday and prompted evacuations of other nearby facilities as authorities fought to get the blaze under control.

video:video.video:title: People in earthquake-hit Philippines city call for help to meet basic needs
video:video.video:description: The mayor of a quake-hit southern Philippine town is pleading for air force helicopters to transport food to stave off hunger in places isolated by landslides.

video:video.video:title: Strong winds damage buildings and tear trees in Wisconsin as US Midwest braces for severe storms
video:video.video:description: Aerial footage shows cleanup crew cleaning up debris, roofs torn off buildings and trees split in half after a strong storm swept through a Wisconsin city.

video:video.video:title: Thousands continue to protest Trump family-linked resort in Albania
video:video.video:description: Thousands of citizens gathered in Tirana for a 12th consecutive day of protests against a proposed luxury resort linked to Jared Kushner, the son-in-law of U.S.

video:video.video:title: Trump believes Iran's Supreme Leader has signed off on emerging deal
video:video.video:description: "I understand the answer is yes" President Donald Trump said when asked by reporters if Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei has agreed to the deal aimed at winding down the conflict.

video:video.video:title: The top photos of the week by Associated Press Photojournalists
video:video.video:description: This photo gallery highlights some of the most compelling images from around the world, published by The Associated Press during the week of 5-11 June 2026

video:video.video:title: NATO chief learns of UK defense secretary's resignation during news conference
video:video.video:description: NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte, informed of Healey's resignation by The Associated Press during a news conference in Brussels, said that Healey is someone “I respect very much.”

video:video.video:title: Ian McKellen honors home of Laurence Olivier
video:video.video:description: One British acting great, Ian McKellen, unveils a blue plaque on the childhood home of another, Sir Laurence Olivier, at 22 Lupus Street in Pimlico, London.

video:video.video:title: Pope slams world's indifference to migrants while visiting onetime 'dock of shame' in Canary Islands
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo on Thursday issued an impassioned plea to recognize the dignity of migrants from the port of Arguineguín, in the Canary Islands. (AP video by Silvia Stellacci)

video:video.video:title: Two-day-old baby elephant plays with ball on eve of World Cup
video:video.video:description: A baby Asian elephant made his debut at a UK zoo on Wednesday at just two days old. On the eve of the FIFA World Cup, the baby boy was given a ball to play with and could be seen attempting a few dribbles and investigating with his trunk.

video:video.video:title: Man pleads guilty to killing a top Minnesota Democrat and her husband, avoids death penalty
video:video.video:description: U.S. Attorney David Rosen said seeking the death penalty was taken off the table once Vance Boelter agreed to plead guilty and serve multiple consecutive life sentences.

video:video.video:title: Mexico fans gather at foot of Angel of Independence after World Cup win
video:video.video:description: Mexico City's iconic Ángel de la Independencia became the center of celebrations Thursday night after Mexico defeated South Africa 2-0 in the opening match of the 2026 World Cup.

video:video.video:title: Mourners gather outside Bangkok hospital where Princess Bajrakitiyabha died
video:video.video:description: Thailand’s Princess Bajrakitiyabha Mahidol, a lawyer and the eldest of King Maha Vajiralongkorn’s children, has died at 47, the Bureau of the Royal Household said.

video:video.video:title: Japan's flagship H3 rocket returns to flight with debut of low-cost variant
video:video.video:description: Japan’s H3 rocket returned to flight Friday with the debut of a new low-cost variant. It took off from the Tanegashima Space Center on a southwestern Japanese island and its second stage successfully reached a targeted orbit, the Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency said.

video:video.video:title: Taylor Swift arrives for the Songwriters Hall of Fame gala
video:video.video:description: Taylor Swift walked the red carpet at the Songwriters Hall of Fame gala in New York. At 36, she's the youngest woman to be inducted. (June 11)

video:video.video:title: Taylor Swift, Kenny Loggins, Alanis Morissette arrive at Songwriters Hall of Fame ceremony
video:video.video:description: Taylor Swift, Kiss' Gene Simmons and Paul Stanley, Christopher “Tricky” Stewart, Alanis Morissette and Kenny Loggins make up the 2026 Songwriters Hall of Fame inductees.

video:video.video:title: Pottery wheel becomes lifeline for blind war veteran in Ukraine
video:video.video:description: A Ukrainian war veteran who was blinded in combat is now teaching others pottery, a craft he learned during rehabilitation.

video:video.video:title: World Cup opener offers those displaced in Gaza and Lebanon an escape from hardship
video:video.video:description: In both Khan Younis and Beirut, those displaced by violence gathered to watch the opening game, as a way of escaping the pressures of war.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories June 12
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Friday, June 12th: Trump calls off strikes on Iran; Israeli strike on southern Lebanon; Planned nitrogen gas execution in Alabama blocked by court; Severe weather in Chicago-area.

video:video.video:title: Supreme Court nixes Alabama request for nitrogen execution, which lower court ruled unconstitutional
video:video.video:description: Supreme Court nixes Alabama request for nitrogen execution, which lower court ruled unconstitutional

video:video.video:title: Taylor Swift teary at Songwriters induction
video:video.video:description: As Taylor Swift became the youngest woman ever inducted into the Songwriters Hall of Fame at 36, she thanked her parents for supporting her and gave advice to young songwriters.

video:video.video:title: Chemistry sparks in 'Your Fault: London'
video:video.video:description: "Your Fault: London" stars Asha Banks and Matthew Broome return to the forbidden romance sequel closer than ever.

video:video.video:title: Swift's emotional speech
video:video.video:description: As Taylor Swift became the youngest woman ever inducted into the Songwriters Hall of Fame, she welled up when thanking her parents and also gave advice to young songwriters.

video:video.video:title: Bosnian song becomes a World Cup banger
video:video.video:description: Bosnian band Dubioza Kolektiv has transformed a song about disillusionment with the American Dream into a viral anthem powering Bosnia and Herzegovina's World Cup dreams.
video:video.video:tag: Bosnia and Herzegovina National Soccer Team

video:video.video:title: Peru police dress up as World Cup mascots during dramatic drugs raid
video:video.video:description: Peru's police have become well known for their targeted stings wearing eye-catching outfits. On Wednesday, they dressed up as 2026 World Cup mascots as they targeted a suspected drug trafficker.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo wraps up his week long visit to Spain
video:video.video:description: The Pope brought to Spain a message of integration during the final mass on Friday, and also had a strong message for the traffickers exploiting the desperation of migrants to make money.

video:video.video:title: SpaceX goes public in historic Wall Street debut
video:video.video:description: SpaceX began trading on Wall Street on Friday in what is expected to be the largest stock market debut in history, a milestone that could make Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire.

video:video.video:title: Trump calls off latest threats to strike Iran, citing a breakthrough in talks to end the war
video:video.video:description: Trump said in the Oval Office on Thursday that a deal to end the war is close, without giving details.

video:video.video:title: Moment between Pope Leo XIV and a Barcelona boy stirred emotion and a social media campaign
video:video.video:description: When Pope Leo XIV locked eyes with 7-year-old Joaquim and held both of his hands as they smiled at each other in the popemobile, the boy’s parents felt it was a direct answer to their prayers.

video:video.video:title: Israel carries out more strikes, controlled demolitions in southern Lebanon despite shaky ceasefire
video:video.video:description: Israel kept pressing on its offensive in Southern Lebanon on Friday, carrying out more strikes as well as controlled demolitions in some of the villages, according to Lebanon's National News Agency.

video:video.video:title: What rising UFO curiosity could mean for religion
video:video.video:description: Are they aliens or demons? As public fascination with UFOs becomes more mainstream, experts and people of faith are increasingly grappling with what that might mean for religion on Earth.

video:video.video:title: David Hockney dies at 88
video:video.video:description: Acclaimed British artist, David Hockney, whose paintings of pools shimmering in the Los Angeles sunshine became icons of 20th-century art, has died at 88. (June 12)

video:video.video:title: Displaced Palestinians in Gaza scramble to salvage belongings after Israeli strikes
video:video.video:description: A block of buildings was reduced to rubble in the areas of Deir al-Balah and Maghazi refugee camp, where residents had been ordered by Israeli forces to temporarily evacuate.

video:video.video:title: Trump says US has reached a 'great settlement' to end war with Iran
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Thursday the U.S. is close to signing "a great settlement" with Iran to wind down the war, with a memorandum of understanding to be signed "over the next few days," likely in Europe.

video:video.video:title: German nonprofit promotes organ donation with tattoo campaign at parliament
video:video.video:description: In Germany, where thousands of patients have remained on transplant waiting lists, a non-profit organization is turning support for organ donation into something permanent: a tattoo.

video:video.video:title: White House previews the cage-match arena for upcoming UFC spectacle
video:video.video:description: The White House hosts an official media walk through of the South Lawn where a UFC mixed martial arts cage-match will be held for Trump’s 80th birthday.

video:video.video:title: Fire destroys medical equipment warehouse in California and prompts evacuations at nearby facilities
video:video.video:description: A fire destroyed a medical equipment warehouse in Northern California and sent thick black smoke pouring into the sky and prompted evacuations of other nearby facilities as authorities fought to get the blaze under control.

video:video.video:title: Elon Musk is officially a trillionaire. But what does $1,000,000,000,000 look like?
video:video.video:description: Elon Musk has shattered global financial history as the world's first trillionaire. Driven by the monumental public market debut of SpaceX, the tech mogul has officially crossed into unprecedented 13-figure wealth.

video:video.video:title: Former USMNT player Carlos Bocanegra shares what the US players will be feeling before the game
video:video.video:description: Former USMNT player Carlos Bocanegra discusses what the US players will be feeling today before the game and the expectations on them.

video:video.video:title: Tornado leaves trail of destruction in Merrillville, Indiana
video:video.video:description: Officials in Merrillville, Indiana said more than 200 buildings were damaged after a strong line of storms barreled through communities south of Chicago on Thursday.

video:video.video:title: Clashes in Kinshasa as protesters reject changes to constitution
video:video.video:description: Violent clashes erupted in the capital of the Democratic Republic of Congo on Friday during a rally against a proposed constitutional change.
video:video.video:tag: Democratic Republic of the Congo government

video:video.video:title: What to know about Patagonia's lawsuit against drag queen Pattie Gonia
video:video.video:description: The clothing brand Patagonia is suing drag queen Pattie Gonia, both of which are popular climate advocates.

video:video.video:title: Díaz-Canel announces economic reforms to attract investment, involve Cubans abroad
video:video.video:description: Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel announced Friday a package of economic reforms aimed at attracting investment, expanding participation by Cubans living abroad in the economy and decentralizing parts of the country’s administration.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo makes flight home from Tenerife aboard Spanish king's jet after plane problem delay
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo's Iberia charter, due to take him back to Rome after a weeklong visit to Spain, was grounded by a technical problem Friday, prompting Spain's king to offer his private jet instead.

video:video.video:title: Trump's name to be removed from Kennedy Center after judge denies last-minute move to keep it
video:video.video:description: A judge cleared the way for President Donald Trump’s name to be removed from the Kennedy Center’s exterior on Friday, denying a last-minute request from the institution’s board that would have kept the name in place.

video:video.video:title: Crews prepare to remove Trump's name from Kennedy Center
video:video.video:description: Construction crews prepared to remove President Donald Trump’s name from the Kennedy Center Friday after a judge rejected a last-minute request from the board to keep it in place.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories June 12
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Friday, June 12th: Police say gunman dead in Texas mass shooting; Midwest residents grapple with tornado damage; Pakistani prime minister says U.S.- Iran deal is nearing; American and Canadian fans catch World Cup fever.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV forced to disembark flight back to Rome from Tenerife due to apparent plane fault
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo's return to Rome from Tenerife was delayed on Friday as he disembarked his plane shortly after boarding due to a technical fault.

video:video.video:title: Suspect dead after Texas shooting kills 1 and injures at least 9 others
video:video.video:description: A shooter who opened fire in the West Texas city of Midland died Friday after a standoff with police following an attack that left one person dead and at least nine others injured, city officials said.

video:video.video:title: Elon Musk is the world's first trillionaire. But what is a trillion dollars?
video:video.video:description: Elon Musk became the world’s first trillionaire after shares of his rocket company SpaceX soared in Wall Street’s biggest initial public offering of stock.

video:video.video:title: Why you should wear earplugs to your next concert
video:video.video:description: Wearing earplugs at concerts can help prevent hearing damage. Starting at around 85 decibels, hearing can be damaged with prolonged listening.

video:video.video:title: AP Reporter recalls flying commercial with former President Joe Biden
video:video.video:description: AP political reporter Meg Kinnard recalls flying on a commercial flight with Former President Joe Biden from Reagan National Airport and ending up stuck in the same weather delays as other passengers.

video:video.video:title: SpaceX stock soars in Wall Street debut and makes Elon Musk the first trillionaire, AP Explains
video:video.video:description: Elon Musk became the world’s first trillionaire on Friday after shares of his rocket company SpaceX soared in Wall Street's biggest initial public offering of stock.

video:video.video:title: Israeli strikes on Tyre leave homes, infrastructure and ancient site in ruins
video:video.video:description: The port city of Tyre in southern Lebanon has been targeted by near-daily Israeli airstrikes that have leveled entire buildings and forced many residents to flee to safer areas elsewhere in Lebanon.

video:video.video:title: 'It will not work': Putin says Ukraine drones won't affect morale
video:video.video:description: Russian President Vladimir Putin held a meeting Friday with Russian servicemen fighting in Ukraine. In the meeting, he accused Russia's "enemies" of creating "division within Russian society."

video:video.video:title: Fans arrive for USA vs. Paraguay FIFA World Cup match
video:video.video:description: Fans arrive as the United States opens its home World Cup match against Paraguay at SoFi Stadium, ending Paraguay's 16-year absence from the tournament.

video:video.video:title: Spain king offers pope lift on royal plane after papal flight grounded
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo's Iberia charter, due to take him back to Rome after a weeklong visit to Spain, was grounded by a technical problem Friday, prompting Spain's king to offer his private jet instead.

video:video.video:title: Soccer legend Beckham gets star on Hollywood Walk of Fame
video:video.video:description: Soccer legend David Beckham received a star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame on Friday. As the World Cup dominates sports headlines, Beckham is largely credited with helping broaden the popularity and reach of the sport in the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Paris Hilton on football creating global unity at FIFA World Cup Opening Ceremony
video:video.video:description: Paris Hilton looks forward to one day becoming a soccer mom and says it's magical the way the sport brings people together across the globe. (June 12)

video:video.video:title: Fans celebrate in Sarajevo as Bosnia draws in first World Cup match
video:video.video:description: Soccer fans celebrated in Sarajevo on Friday as they watched their national team draw 1-1 against co-host nation Canada in their group's opening match in the World Cup tournament, where Bosnia-Herzegovina is making only its second appearance, a goal that once seemed improbable as more traditional so
video:video.video:tag: Bosnia and Herzegovina National Soccer Team

video:video.video:title: Canada fans out in full force for 1st-ever World Cup match in Toronto
video:video.video:description: Thousands of red-clad supporters enthusiastically backed Canada’s team on soccer’s biggest stage (AP video by Mike Householder)

video:video.video:title: How much do fans know about the FIFA World Cup?
video:video.video:description: How much do fans know about the FIFA World Cup? They were put to the test ahead of the United States vs. Paraguay match. #WorldCup #2026WorldCup #FIFAWorldCup #Socce
